mission.title: tikv: Refine PD client
mission.description: Refine PD client. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current tikv deployment
steps.description:
Verify your tiup --version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
helm list -n tikv 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working tikv installation.

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv
```
## Development Task

PD client (and PD worker) has been too complicated to be maintained in the past years. There are subtle bugs due to its complicated reconnect mechanism and lock usage.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Refine PD client
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Refine PD client" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #13673 

What's Changed:

- Move reconnect logic to one single coroutine `reconnect_loop`
- Move streaming RPCs to individual long-running coroutines that are not destroyed even when connection is rebuilt
- All client methods take mutable reference now
- `PdConnector` and some other constants are shared with v1
- Old client tests are

See the source issue and linked PR for the complete solution details.

prerequisites.description: A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV.
